title: Dental practitioners
description: Examine, diagnose, and treat diseases, injuries, and malformations of teeth and gums. May treat diseases of nerve, pulp, and other dental tissues affecting oral hygiene and retention of teeth. May fit dental appliances or provide preventive care. Perform surgery and related procedures on the hard and soft tissues of the oral and maxillofacial regions to treat diseases, injuries, or defects. May diagnose problems of the oral and maxillofacial regions. May perform surgery to improve function or appearance. Examine, diagnose, and treat dental malocclusions and oral cavity anomalies. Design and fabricate appliances to realign teeth and jaws to produce and maintain normal function and to improve appearance. (Based on 5 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Administer anesthetics, both general and local, to limit the amount of pain experienced by patients during procedures. This includes using masks, gloves, and safety glasses to protect both patients and staff from infectious diseases.

Measure and take impressions of patients' jaws and teeth to determine the shape and size of dental prostheses, and fit dental appliances in patients' mouths to alter the position and relationship of teeth and jaws to realign teeth.
Repair, reline, or rebase dentures and replace missing teeth with permanent fixtures or removable appliances.

Restore function and aesthetics to traumatic injury survivors or individuals with diseases or congenital disabilities, and provide cosmetic treatments such as placing veneers or bleaching discolored teeth.
Treat facial pain and jaw joint problems, and perform surgery on the mouth and jaws to resolve conditions such as cleft lip and palate, or to remove impacted and damaged teeth.
Collaborate with other professionals to plan treatment and advise patients on plans, preventive care, and the causes and treatment of dental problems.
Perform surgery to prepare the mouth for dental implants, treat infections of the oral cavity, and address other conditions, including removal of tumors and abnormal growths.
Evaluate the position of the wisdom teeth and diagnose diseases, injuries, or malformations of teeth and gums while examining patients using diagnostic equipment.
Analyze or evaluate dental needs to determine changes or trends in patterns of dental disease, and study diagnostic records to develop treatment plans.
Fill pulp chambers and canals with endodontic materials, treat problems affecting the oral mucosa, and provide emergency treatment for facial injuries.
Design and fabricate dental prostheses or supervise dental technicians who construct these devices, including creating and fitting prosthodontic appliances such as bridges or dentures.
Provide patients with proposed treatment plans and cost estimates while managing business aspects of the practice, including employing staff and handling paperwork.
Instruct dental officers and technical assistants in orthodontic procedures and coordinate orthodontic services with other dental and medical services.
Perform minor cosmetic procedures and facial rejuvenation techniques as supplemental services, such as laser surgeries.
Plan and maintain dental health programs and produce or evaluate educational materials about dental health.
Adjust dental appliances as necessary to produce and maintain normal function in patients, ensuring proper fitting and modifications are made.
Provide fluoride treatments or sealants, clean or polish teeth to restore their natural color, and eliminate irritating margins of fillings or correct occlusions.

Evaluate and treat problems related to the temperomandibular joint (TMJ).
